cards.purpose: a 'Prompt' panel that types its text out over the clip; [m:ss] timestamp tags glow orange, a caret blinks, the box auto-scrolls — for showing an AI-tool/video prompt being written

variables.title: string
variables.prompt: string — the full prompt, \n for line breaks; [m:ss] tags auto-highlight

cards.purpose: hero for one tool/model: a glassy app-icon tile with the tool's (muted) logo, its name beneath, and an optional subtitle — the 'this is the tool' beat
vertical checklist; each row's box fills and checkmark draws in as it reveals on its beat
horizontal bar chart racing from zero to each product's value

variables.title: string
variables.bars: array of {label, value, color?, prefix?, suffix?} — ALWAYS carry the unit on each bar (prefix '$' / suffix 'ms'); missing colors fall back to the card palette

cards.purpose: monthly vs yearly plan cards side by side with a savings stamp on the yearly plan
cart summary panel where a coupon code applies and the total counts down
comparison table over a storm/particle background; rows cascade in and the winner column glows

variables.rows: array of {feature, values: one per tool}

cards.purpose: S/A/B/C/F tier list ranking tools with logo pills sliding into each tier row
feature comparison matrix; each row of check/cross/value cells reveals on its beat

beat_shape.name: string
beat_shape.values: array, EXACTLY one per product, in products order: true/false for checks or a short string

cards.purpose: two-contender VS panel with stats stacked on each side and a center VS badge
2-3 column pricing plan cards with one plan flagged as recommended

variables.heading: string
variables.plans: array of {name, price, monthlyPrice?, billingNote?, period, tagline, features: string[], cta, best: boolean}

cards.purpose: full-price bar that slices off discount chunks one by one down to a final price
scored comparison matrix with a reason column; each factor row reveals on its beat

variables.productLogos: array of strings (optional) — logos/ registry slugs matching products, e.g. ['heygen', 'openart']

beat_shape.name: string
beat_shape.values: array of score strings, EXACTLY one per product in products order (e.g. "4.5 / 5")
beat_shape.reason: string (optional) — one-line why, shown in the last column

cards.purpose: chroma-key subscribe button overlay that rings the bell and presses on click
chroma-key pill overlay pointing viewers to the link in the description
transparent overlay: a curved arrow draws from a label toward whatever you're pointing at
transparent tip/warning/note callout box that pops in over screen recording
transparent overlay decoding a redacted coupon code with a copy-flash sweep
transparent overlay slamming in a discounted price with a strike-through original and save stamp
transparent lower-third name + subtitle pill sliding in at bottom-left
transparent overlay punching in a big savings amount next to a round seal
spoken score badge: label + animated N/10 pill (optional logo; gold only when winner) at the second the score is said
transparent overlay dimming the screen except a highlighted region, with a click ripple inside it
transparent step-progress pill (Step X of Y) with a progress-dot row for tutorial footage
transparent full-width tip/warning banner sliding in along the bottom or top edge
spoken pros/cons pinned to footage: green-check / rose-cross pill chips tick in one per beat and persist, anchored bottom-left
two-column pros vs cons; each row reveals on its beat
plain numbered agenda list; each row reveals on its beat
re-shown table-of-contents that dims done/upcoming items and highlights the current section
end-of-video recap; each takeaway ticks in with a check badge on its beat
section divider where a glass card flips up from below and settles with a spring bounce
section divider where a big number counts up then scales down as the title and dots ripple in

variables.title: string
variables.logo: string (optional) — logos/ registry slug, e.g. 'heygen'; shown beside the title

cards.purpose: section divider with drifting light streaks and particles behind an elastic title pop
numbered table-of-contents list; each row reveals on its beat
title card with drifting aurora blobs and a light bar that wipes logo chips into view — THE cold-open pick for multi-product comparisons (products become the visual hero)

variables.title: string
variables.platforms: array of {name, logo? (logos/ registry slug)} — the chips the light bar wipes in; name-only entries render as text chips. Omit entirely = demo chips (never ship those)

cards.purpose: title card with a breathing vignette background; title scales in and uniform tool tiles cascade then gently bob — a strong comparison cold-open (products are the hero, every tile identical size)

variables.title: string
variables.platforms: array of {name, logo? (logos/ registry slug)} — uniform glass tiles the logos sit in at a fixed centered footprint; name-only entries render as text tiles. Omit = demo chips (never ship those)

cards.purpose: title card with kinetic line-by-line text reveal for kicker, title, and subtitle
title card with a storm background and the title popping in with an elastic overshoot
verdict card where the winner slides in and five achievement badges pop in one by one
verdict report card with a glowing letter grade; each sub-grade reveals on its beat
verdict card with confetti and a trophy dropping in before the winner card lifts with a gold pulse. ONE winner per card — a dual verdict gets one card per winner, back to back

variables.logo: string (optional) — logos/ registry slug, shown beside the winner name

cards.purpose: big animated stat: count-up hero number + label + context line, transparent overlay for demo footage
section opener for one tool/product: TOOL N OF M progress, huge name, positioning line, category chip

side-by-side verdict winners over a confetti stage, one tool column per beat; scales 2-4 winners (layout derives from count). Use for a multi-winner finale — co-winners (ranked:false, all gold) or a ranked podium (ranked:true → gold/silver/bronze/steel). ONE tool per beat.

variables.ranked: boolean (optional, default false) — false: every column an equal gold co-winner; true: medals by beat order (1st gold, 2nd silver, 3rd bronze, 4th steel)

beat_shape.name: string — one tool/product name
beat_shape.reason: string — one-line why this tool won

cards.purpose: use-case verdict: persona → winning tool pairs, one pair per beat
credit/unit economics rows: plan → credits → what that actually buys, one row per beat
numbered step flow: process walkthrough nodes revealing left to right, one step per beat
comparison table that builds one row per beat as each product's numbers are spoken; cells pipe-separated in beat text

variables.title: string
variables.columns: array of strings — header cells, 2-4 columns

beat_shape.logo: string (optional) — logos/ registry slug, shown muted before the first cell

cards.purpose: canvas beat: a spoken headline sentence plus pill chips ticking in one per beat (lists, consequences, criteria)
one spoken sentence alone on the ambient canvas; words appear as spoken and an accent phrase colorizes with them; used INSTEAD of a talking-head bridge

variables.accent: string (optional) — a phrase appearing verbatim inside text; those words render in --accent
